items.title: Nvidia Stock Closed Tuesday Down 6% After Hitting All-Time Highs: What Happened?
items.author: Vaishali Prayag
items.description: Nvidia shares briefly hit new all-time highs after CEO Jensen Huang's CES 2025 keynote, but closed Tuesday down over 6% amid broader market weakness following economic data.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Nvidia shares hit new highs but closed down 6% due to broader market weakness, despite the company's new product announcements at CES 2025.
The SPDR S&P 500 ETF closed down 1.12% on Tuesday, contributing to the broader market weakness that weighed on Nvidia's stock price.

items.title: US Stocks Could Open On A Tentative Note; Nvidia, AMD And Toyota On Investors' Radar: Experts Flag Over Reliance On AI, Tech Stocks
items.author: Benzinga Senior Editor
items.description: U.S. stocks could open on a tentative note as investors remain cautious about the market's reliance on AI and tech stocks. Experts warn that this overreliance could lead to a market correction or a bear market in the Nasdaq if a market rotation to other sectors does not occur soon.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Nvidia shares rose 3.4% on Monday, marking one of the session's strongest performances among chipmakers.
Chipmakers like AMD led the rally on Monday after Foxconn reported record fourth-quarter revenue.
Micron Technology surged 10.5% on Monday, marking one of the session's strongest performances among chipmakers.
Toyota is mentioned as one of the companies on investors' radar, but no specific information is provided about the company's performance or outlook.
The SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust edged up by 0.12% in premarket trading on Tuesday.
The Invesco QQQ ETF declined 0.03% in premarket trading on Tuesday.
